top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 82, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
66ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At 5 Mila Marche Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of 5 Mila Marche Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
5 Mila Marche Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 82 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic 5 Mila Marche Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 82 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
5,446ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
5,446ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 82 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 82 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At 5 Mila Marche Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.heat: September in Porto Recanati, Marche, Italy can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before 5 Mila Marche Gran Fondo. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for 5 Mila Marche Gran Fondo’s 82 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at 5 Mila Marche Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at 5 Mila Marche Gran Fondo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Over Under to prepare you for 82 miles of mixed terrain in Porto Recanati, Marche, Italy.
